text.text:
We’ve been coming to Siloso Beach Resort since 10 years ago or more — from 2 kids and now 3! It’s surrounded by lush greenery with a lovely spring water pool.

We truly appreciate that the resort provides opportunities for staff with special needs. Do be patient and kind — they are doing their best, and it makes a difference. The team is warm, and the owner is extremely humble.

It may not be a 5-star hotel, but it’s peaceful, calming, and perfect to unwind. Best part — the beach is just across the resort, a 30 seconds walk away. We’ll always come back ❤️

text.text:
Location is superb. Hotel is in a walking distance to the Siloso beach.
We stayed in a family room with 4 single beds. The room was spacious with a nice view of the rain forest trees and the pool was amazing.
Downsides were the power points locations in the room. we had just 2 power point in the most awkward location. one under the kitchen bench and one on the shelving cabinet. None near the beds.
We have been told that the hotel doesn’t provide face and hand towels and we stuck with bath towels, one per each for our stay.
Towels were old and some had  none pleasant smell.
Smoking area was near the lifts, so you smell it when you wanted to take the lift. Not a proper location.
Around the pool, the towel station was unattended most of the time and sometimes it had no towel.

text.text: Despite the slightly old school architecture, the enormous protected cool pool becomes a sanctuary away from the extreme warm and noise of the beach clubs. Next door there’s a 7-11 open until late. It’s a non-pretentious non-assuming hotel with decent breakfast and a social approach to staffing. Highly recommended for a short break in Sentosa.

Complaints: Some guests found the power points in their room inconveniently located, and there was an issue with towels being old and having unpleasant smells. One reviewer also noted that the smoking area near the lifts caused a strong smell.

Top notch experience with Rina and her team. From pre check in, asking for special occasions to receiving us at the hotel, they certainly have set the bars high. Im sure they can't do it for everyone but i hope they continue the hard work. Even the concierge teams were great at engaging my baby when we arrived.

Rooms were very clean, the deluxe room still had space for a cot. View given was a directly facing the pool as i requested for a quiet room and it was far in the middle. Super lots of rooms and Rina arranged late check out for us.

My ideal type of trip where so much rest was given. No rush and no worries for this staycation. Thank you.

text.text:
Had a lovely staycation here for the third time, and once again it did not disappoint. Special thanks to Tivya at the reception — she was wonderful and thoughtfully arranged a room with a bathtub as requested, and also managed to secure a higher-floor room for us, which we really appreciated.

The room was very clean and spotless. The air-conditioning was a little noisy on the medium fan setting, but switching it to low made it much quieter and comfortable enough to sleep through the night.

The swimming pool is clean and well maintained, and very kid-friendly with a children’s wading pool and a lifeguard on duty, which gave us peace of mind. The Christmas booths at the hotel lobby added a warm and festive vibe to the stay.

This is our third time staying here, and we will definitely be back again. My kids enjoyed it a lot, making it a great choice for families.

text.text:
Location of 7-11 is very convenient!!

Rina managed to get us a great room. Marvellous view, facing the pool. She promptly directed us and even provided short-cuts to navigate around the hotel.

During check-in, the concierge — Diva & Adilah, were very warm and welcoming to my nephew. They treated my nephew with utmost care. Diva even provided a bag of colouring booklet and birthday balloons to my nephew!

Ashwini was very welcoming and friendly towards my nephew throughout our stay.

The tall Chinese uncle at the towel station was friendly and very accommodating to me and my nephew. He inflated a pool floatie and provided a water gun for my nephew too! It was a very pleasant interaction with him!

The staff at the breakfast counter, Sherry, were pleasant and very friendly. It was great to be greeted with such warmth in the mornings I had during the stay!

Upon checking out, I had issues in booking a taxi. Nabil was very helpful in placing a booking for us.

Razak frequently checked up on us to ensure that we were comfortable. He provided many recommendations and was swift with providing us what we needed.

Room for Improvement:
Please enlarge the symbols of pork on your food tags. I nearly consumed pork as the symbol was barely noticeable. I understand that Native Kitchen is not a fully Halal Certified dining restaurant, but I hope that VHS is able to make these changes to accommodate to their Muslim guests.

Tiles in the swimming pools should be replaced. The broken tiles are sharp and pose a risk to cuts. Hope that VHS will repair the tiles as soon as possible.

text.text:
Had a family staycation 2 weeks ago and it was a very enjoyable experience. Never had such a personalised customer service experience. From a week before my stay, they sent a Whatsapp to remind me about my stay and to ask what time is my ETA. They also provided a packing list based on what guests could do in the hotel and in Sentosa. A few days before my stay, Rina asked if I was celebrating any special occasion. On the day itself, Rina was all ready to receive us. In fact she had our room prepared to welcome my little ones, with balloons, snacks and activity kit greeting us.

Our room was facing the sea and we could see fireworks from the Wings of Times. We also enjoyed the movie screening on Fri and Sat night. Next to the hotel, there was also another movie screening by Movies by the Beach. The pools were so amazing! We enjoyed the pools so much!

The hotel is conveniently located next to Imbiah Station (5min walk away) and is linked to designated walkway to where all the beaches are.

Food wise, Vivo has a lot of food options. We were surprised that the food delivery is not so expensive as well.

Lovely place and lovely experience overall, and would definitely come back!

text.text: We had a wonderful stay from start to finish. The check-in and check-out processes were incredibly smooth and efficient. The food was consistently excellent, and the staff were exceptionally warm and helpful throughout our visit. We especially appreciated the thoughtful anniversary surprises—the cake, wine, and flowers were a beautiful touch. Having stayed at Shangri-La properties from the Philippines to Singapore, we are always impressed by the high standard of service.

text.text:
Firstly, service here is top notch! It's always pleasant and welcoming, from Kathleen Grace of Front Office, Tara of Business Lounge, Leo of Pool Activities and every staff throughout our stay. Rasa Sentosa has always been our choice resort on Sentosa for its service, food quality, private beach & its activities though we couldn't be near the beach this time due to sightings of crocodile :(
Resort is perfectly located at the end of Siloso beach, close to the cable car, tram and buses, thou the resort has shuttle across to the mainland too.
The rooms have been refurbished and I miss the louver windows (maybe just me) thou its replaced with very thick blackout curtains.  Appreciated some of the eco initiatives such as aircon in the room shutting off when balcony doors are opened and turns it on when door's shut.  And among the comprehensive provision of toiletries, the good size toothpaste is packed separately from the toothbrush which was also a good size one...no waste of another toothbrush just to get to another tiny tube of toothpaste.
Room is well equipped with sufficient international power points, plentiful of drinking water and coffee & tea supplies, beach slippers and even a pretty tote bag. The water & ice dispenser in the corridor of every floor is godsend in this tropical resort.
Unfortunately, our sleep wasn't great.  Bed & pillows were uncomfortable. Sheets rustled "noisily" when tugged.  Also, TV resolution and bluetooth speaker on the side table were so terrible that they instantly make the resort felt cheap :(
Hope to return soon to a better experience.

text.text: We loved our stay with Shangri-la Sentosa. The rooms were beautiful, clean and really well equipped. The accommodation staff were so attentive and friendly. The breakfast was amazing with a great selection. Amenities and pools were fantastic. There are fun little activities like SUP, kayaks and pedal boats on the private beach. Everyone was so helpful and always had a smile. Its also very convenient to jump on the shuttle or the cable cars and get around the island. Would highly recommend.

text.text:
We stayed at Shangri-La Rasa Sentosa for 11 days and had a wonderful time. The service was spectacular, everyone was friendly, attentive, and made the stay feel effortless.

We also used room service, which was convenient and consistently good. A really nice touch was the small goodies and little treats they left in our room,
those thoughtful details made us feel very welcomed.

It’s also incredibly family-oriented with lots to do for kids: a kids’ club, a kids’ pool, and plenty of activities, including on the beach. Our little ones enjoyed every single day during our stay.

Breakfast was another highlight, fantastic with many different options and something for everyone. Overall, a great choice for families and we’d happily return.

text.text:
Dear Management Team at Shangri-La Rasa Sentosa,
I am writing to share my feedback following a wonderful 3-day, 2-night stay at your resort. We had a fantastic experience and truly enjoyed the relaxing beachfront atmosphere.
I would like to especially highlight two members of your team who made our stay exceptional:
Jennifer Chung: Her professionalism, warmth, and attentiveness were truly impressive. She went above and beyond to ensure we were well taken care of.
Zul (Lifeguard): We really appreciated Zul’s friendly and approachable demeanor at the pool. It’s reassuring to see a safety professional who is not only vigilant but also so welcoming to guests.
Both Jennifer and Zul are a credit to the Shangri-La brand. Please ensure this praise is shared with them and their respective department heads.
Other highlights of our stay included:
The excellent breakfast spread and variety at Silver Shell Cafe.
The well-maintained pool facilities and the seamless beach access.
The overall "island escape" vibe that made our short getaway feel much longer.
Thank you for the great hospitality. We are already looking forward to our next visit!
Warm regards,
Hareedas

They appreciated thoughtful touches like anniversary surprises and eco-friendly initiatives. However, some complained about uncomfortable beds and pillows, poor TV resolution, and high prices. Suitable for families, couples, and those seeking a luxurious island getaway with convenient access to amenities and activities.

text.text:
I am sorry to write this review and I really wanted to like this place but I just could not.

I paid 5 star price for a 3 star quality hotel. No bell boy when we arrived at the hotel after 10pm. We had to drag our massive suitcases down, some stairs then onto elevator. The staff at reception were nice but NOT the 5 star service I was expecting. No proper welcome into their hotel like you expect to feel. The hotel/resort gave out no 5 star vibes. The breakfast was below average. I do have to mention that a young Indian ethnicity looking gentleman during breakfast may have been the only staff who had excellent service and communication but everyone else fell short from what I had expected.

The rooms were of 3.5 star to 4 star quality. I was so disappointed only because I paid over 750AUD per night for an absolute below average hotel. Often when I called reception for waters, there was no answer. On our departure date I called multiple times to no avail and I was forced to walk down to lobby to request for bell boy.

My apologies for this review once again but paying so much really left a dent on this experience.

I had an opportunity to work in the yacht industry for part of my career path. And I was lucky to meet an elderly couple (they were probably in their 70s) who had passed on a very important lesson to me - managing our emotions.

We had an event with some car brands and they came in an entourage. It was a nice event, everybody had a great time. But they had some issues with their car and it was past midnight 1 or 2 am? Everybody had left, I stayed with them until help came.

I know they were probably a bit annoyed /tired because it was a really nice car from an established brand. But they remained very calm and collected. Thank you K family 😘

text.text:
I had our solemnization and lunch reception here at the Nova room and we are truly happy with the service and coordination.

Love the location and ambience of the Nova room as well as the location. Additionally, the prompt response from Beck Chin is one of the important aspect we have decided on the location.

Prior to contacting ONE°15 Marina, we have contacted several other hotels. However, most of the time, we were met with slow response, unlike Beck Chin, he has been very prompt in response right from the start of our enquiries till after the event. He has been a great help in coordinating our needs and gave good suggestions along the way.

Perhaps, the room is slightly below our expectation. The lighting is pretty dim and relatively basic. The hefty deposit is rather uncommon as hotels would typically use credit card as guarantee. Additionally, the hotel should consider to provide flexibility  for early check-ins for bridal make up as that would would certainly be helpful for couples having lunch reception.

Again, thanks Beck Chin for the help to create a memorable event for us.

text.text:
Recently held my wedding at ONE°15 Marina and me and the wife's experience was immaculate.

My first contact was with Beck. From the start up till the very end, he was responsive and attentive to our needs. He would reply us almost immediately anytime of the day and would follow up with any of the unknowns and queries expeditiously. Beck is also knowledgeable and sensitive to cultural/religious norms; a major plus for our multicultural wedding affair! Thank you Beck, we had a good feeling after we first met you and we were definitely not wrong!

On our big day, banquet manager Joyston was assigned to us. I had met him the day before for coordination and I must say I was impressed by his grasp on our wedding itinerary. He was able to remember specific details regarding the events! Along with the bridal team, groomsmen and vendors he was able to facilitate a smooth flow of events. Although it was a short engagement with Joyston, we are thankful that we had experienced the amazing service you provided.

The backdrop of the yatches and boats along with the decor provided made for a beautiful venue. The rooms provided for us, though just slightly dated in certain aspects, were very clean and comfortable. Service provided by staff was timely and efficient.

Thank you ONE°15 Marina for an extremely memorable wedding experience :)

summary:
Here's a summary of the hotel reviews:

Guests liked: The location and ambience of the Nova room, the staff's responsiveness and helpfulness, especially from Beck Chin, and the overall peaceful atmosphere. Guests also appreciated the beautiful views of the marina and hillside.

Complaints: Some guests experienced issues with room quality, cleanliness, and lighting, as well as a lack of proper welcome services and poor breakfast options. One guest also mentioned difficulty in getting assistance with luggage due to no bell boy service after 10pm.

text.text:
I had an extremely unpleasant stay at this hotel.

When I checked in, I asked the front desk for an extra blanket for my family. They told me the hotel was fully booked and had no extra duvet available, even though the booking app clearly showed there were still rooms for sale.

The staff also insisted that a twin room only comes with two duvets and that they do not provide extra duvrt as a rule. I tried to explain but got no help.

I then contacted the booking platform’s customer service, who followed up with the hotel. The hotel’s reply was still the same: no extra duvet, not even for a fee.

This is unbelievable and completely unacceptable. It’s the first time I’ve ever heard of a hotel charging for an extra duvet. What’s more, how can a hotel run out of duvets entirely? It’s shocking that a so-called professional hotel has no spare supplies at all.

We are a family of three, including an 8-year-old child. The room was cold at night, and we only needed one more duvet to keep warm. Is that too much to ask?

I used to think Far East Organization was a benchmark in hotel management, but this experience has put this hotel straight on my blacklist. I will not recommend this hotel to anyone.

The staff were exceptional! Very friendly, always happy to help. A special shout out to the dinner team whom remembered us from the first night and made our stay extra special.

Breakfast was great and there is a live egg station where eggs are cooked how you like fresh in front of you. I’ve never seen this before and thought this was a great touch. The breakfast buffet has a good variety of foods and I found it worth the try

The hotel is very nice and has a frequent free shuttle bus that takes guests to and from vivo city as well as the island free shuttle bus outside the hotel. Getting around was easy because of this but it is a bit out of the way and takes a bit of extra time. The beach is a short walk away and the hotel pool was nice. There’s also resident peacocks on the ground giving a relaxed holiday ambience. It was very quiet and relaxing and has good amenities. The hotel is a bit dated here and there. But everything was clean and service was top. We had an amazing time and would stay again.

text.text:
I’ve stayed at many luxury properties, but the service at Sofitel Sentosa is truly in a league of its own. What set this stay apart wasn't just the amenities, but the incredible staff who went above and beyond to make us feel welcome.
Highlights of the Experience:
- Personalised Hospitality: The level of recognition here is astounding. Whether I was heading to breakfast or a late-night drink, the staff consistently greeted me by name. It turned a standard hotel stay into a personalized experience that felt like coming home.
- Dining at Kwee Zeen: The service at Kwee Zeen was particularly amazing. The team was attentive, warm, and seemed to anticipate my needs before I even asked. It made every meal feel like a special occasion.
- Atmosphere at Le Bar: We spent several evenings at Le Bar, and the hospitality there was equally flawless. The staff’s familiarity and friendly demeanor created such a relaxed, upscale environment that we looked forward to returning to every day.

The property itself is stunning, but it’s the people who make this place a gem. They don't just provide a service, they provide genuine care. If you value a stay where you are treated as an individual rather than just a room number, this is the place to be.
We can’t wait to return 😊

We had a wonderful stay at Sofitel Sentosa. The service was exceptional, especially from Hafiz at Concierge, as well as the F&B team at LeBar (Kristie, if I’m not mistaken) and at Kwee Zeen — Pamela, Julie, and Samuel. They were all such a delight. Everyone greeted us warmly with genuine smiles, and nothing was ever too much trouble. Their hospitality truly made our stay memorable.

However, I would like to share some feedback regarding the lady at the Front Office who handled our check-in and check-out. Unfortunately, I can’t recall her name (I’m sure it can be easily identified through your hotel PMS records). She did not come across as friendly — there was no smile or greeting, and on one occasion when we approached the desk, she simply said, “Yes??”

I don’t believe this reflects the standard of service expected at a five-star hotel. It was quite disappointing, especially when compared to the warmth and professionalism shown by the Concierge and F&B teams.

Overall, we truly enjoyed our stay and appreciate the wonderful service from the rest of your team.

(Stay dates: 21-24 February 2026)

text.text:
Check-in was a great start thanks to Shazriena, who was warm and welcoming. The staff overall were actually the highlight of the stay — special thanks as well to AJ, James and MJ who were helpful and professional throughout.

Unfortunately, the hotel itself did not meet the standards expected of a five-star property. Upon entering the room, simple details such as a mirror with visible stains suggested housekeeping had not been thorough. The décor and furniture also felt dated.

Room service was particularly disappointing. I ordered a Caesar salad with extra chicken and when it arrived promptly, I was pleased — until I discovered the chicken, although cooked, was completely frozen inside. The salad, including the bacon and eggs, was ice cold and unpleasant to eat. When I called room service, the response was simply that they would heat it up, which was not an acceptable solution.

There were also odd details in the room such as no hooks to hang towels, and the towels themselves were inconsistent in material, size and even colour, some with loose threads which made them feel more like something from a budget hotel.

At breakfast, staff were using a blower outside to clear leaves while guests were eating, which was disruptive and poorly timed.

Other issues included housekeeping not answering calls during the entire three-day stay, lights inside the wardrobe that would not switch off when the doors were closed, and overall cleanliness and maintenance that could clearly be improved.

The staff were excellent, but the hotel needs to address these operational and quality issues if it wants to live up to its five-star positioning.

text.text:
A Soul-Healing Escape: Beyond Five-Star Service

Despite the resort undergoing some refurbishments during my stay February 24/2/25, the tranquility and ambiance remained completely undisturbed. In fact, the generosity of the staff made the construction feel invisible. From the immaculately kept grounds where peacocks roam freely to the modern, pristine facilities, every corner of this resort radiates care.
My bungalow was nothing short of magical—sipping the signature Amara Singapore tea blend became my favorite way to unwind, and the never-ending supply of daily replenished snacks made me feel truly pampered.

What really sets this place apart, however, is the heart of its people:
The Food: Breakfast was a masterclass in balance between Asian and International flavors. Being gifted a national dish to try was a "healing" experience I didn't know I needed.
The Personal Touch: Sita from the breakfast team surprised me with a dessert from our shared cultures, and the Breakfast Chef prepared a care package for my departure that moved me to tears on my flight. The staff running out to gift me a candle when I left and a personalised recommendation to shop in the Malay district.
The Leadership: I will forever treasure my memories with Shaz, the Duty Manager, who ensured I felt heard and valued throughout. It was Shaz who made me fall in love with Singapore, and I will have her beautiful face etched in my memories.
This wasn't just a stay; it was a connection. The staff remain in my thoughts and prayers.

text.text:
We recently stayed at Oasia Resort Hotel for two days. Our room was a decent size and included a sofa bed, although no extra bedding was provided and it wasn’t made up for us as we’ve experienced at other hotels. That said, the room itself was very nice and comfortable.

Check-in was smooth overall. Prior to arrival, I was sent a form to complete to speed up the process, but I still had to provide all passports and details at the desk, so I’m not sure it actually saved any time—filling it out felt a bit unnecessary.

The room had a hot water dispenser and a Nespresso machine, which was a nice touch. It would have been even better to have a selection of different teas and some milk provided. We didn’t have breakfast at the hotel, so can’t comment on that.

The location is excellent—within walking distance to Imbiah Station, and just one monorail stop from Universal Studios. Everything is very accessible. We didn’t get a chance to use many of the facilities or the pool, but it looked lovely.

Overall, we enjoyed our stay and would choose this hotel again mainly because of its great location. Thank you.

text.text:
We had a really lovely stay at Oasia Resort Sentosa. The reception team were warm and welcoming from the moment we arrived, with friendly faces and a nice tone. Housekeeping was outstanding – Li Li in particular was efficient, quick and very considerate. She did our housekeeping so quietly while our baby was sleeping, and still left the room spotless.
There was a small issue during our stay, but Duty Manager James handled it very well. He stayed calm, resolved it quickly and made sure we were well taken care of for the rest of our stay. Breakfast was also baby-friendly, which made things much easier for us as parents.
The hotel does get very busy during check-in and check-out times as they handle many large foreign tour groups, so it can be hard to reach reception by phone at those times. One small suggestion would be to install a bidet in the bathrooms – it’s a small cost but would be a big added convenience for guests. Overall, we really appreciated the hospitality and would happily come back again.

text.text:
My first Pawretreat 2 nights stay with my snr corgi boy at Oasia Hotel Sentosa fr 6- 8 Jan 2026
Room: Stay in Courtyard Premier, very cosy & gd for my corgi to hang around. However, the awning in full mode makes the courtyard very warm, so requested to drawn it back to halfway to allow air circulation & wind.
Strongly suggest to install a wall fan where the dining table is, makes the area cooler.
Interior was comfy & cosy, pillows were soft and comfortable for sleep. Complimentary coconut drinks was great.
The only problem was the circuit breaker tripped twice on the second day caused by external wall lights affected by the rain.
Hotel staffs were quick to attend to our room blackout and we were offered alternative room on the second blackout.
Surprised they offered complimentary breakfast for the next day for the inconvenience.👏🏻
However, the alternative room had a malfunction awning that cannot be retracted, so court yard was very warm outside.
Location: Very Central & Convenient, just opp Impiah Stn. Easy walk to beach, 8-10 mins walk.
Staff: Friendly, Attentive & Helpful, making our stay special.
On the 3 day check out, the hotel mgr Jess ( very professional & friendly lady) was so apologetic abt the room inconvenience and offered us a complimentary Grabpet ride home. 👏🏻
All the sweet surprises make our pawstaycation so memorable.🥰
Highly recommended for pawretreat in this hotel.

text.text:
I am heartbroken and extremely disappointed with my 3D2N stay at Hotel Michael, which was meant to celebrate my 20th year anniversary and birthday. What should have been a joyful and meaningful occasion was completely spoiled.

The first room was unbearably smelly, and although the reception staff kindly helped me change rooms, the second room was even worse. There were ants everywhere — on my food, towels, and even crawling on my body while I slept, which was truly distressing. It causes my body to have rashes after the stay.  The toilet flush barely worked, the towel was with black stains and the balcony door couldn’t be locked, making me feel uncomfortable and unsafe. Worst still the coffee machine was not working too.

Instead of celebrating, I spent my stay feeling stressed, upset, and unhappy. The only positive thing was the kind and helpful staff, especially at the reception and housekeeping.

I deeply regret choosing this hotel for such an important milestone. No one deserves to have their special moments ruined like this. I just can't believe a 5 stars hotel is like this.

The convenience factor is a major plus. Staff are polite, professional, and helpful, ensuring a smooth check-in and overall pleasant experience. While the hotel doesn’t try to be overly flashy, it delivers consistently on comfort, service, and location.

text.text:
Supposed to check into Jurong but they informed that I've been upgraded and even arranged transport for me to get to hotel Michael. In the midst of it there were a few hiccups - I think the main thing was that the transferred booking didnt go through or got lost in communication. They took a good 30 mins to sort it out at the front desk, thankfully I had a call to take or I would've been quite irritated.

Anyway, 30 mins later I got my room. Walked all the way to the room which was quite a distance even from the lift, the door tag buzzed red when I tagged my key card. Tried both - failed, ended up going back to the front desk. Big thanks to Thiti who helped me with the miscomm and subsequently with the rectification of the wrong key card being passed to me.

Room was good, clean and spacious. Free mini bar was nice and overall enjoyed my stay. They use clarins products in the room. Design/interior was a bit dated but still well kept. They have a drawdown service too in the evening, something I wasnt expecting. Noise insulation can be better in the rooms.

Tip; There is a QR at the counter for Sentosa express for hotel guests.

text.text:
I had an amazing stay at Capella, and the service was truly top-notch. We had a late dinner at Fiamma, ordering just a few drinks and an appetiser. As it was quite late, we decided to take the main course to go. However, the restaurant staff member, Kalai, kindly recommended that the meal be served in our room instead. It was such a thoughtful and considerate gesture.

The next morning, we returned to the same restaurant for breakfast. The food was fantastic, offering a great variety with both a buffet and free-flowing mains. The manager Canny was very friendly, and all the staff were equally welcoming and attentive.

Before leaving, we decided to relax by the pool, where we met Nelvin. He treated us warmly and generously offered ice cream and frozen grapes to help us cool down in the heat. As we departed the property, we were greeted by the bellman, who was also friendly and helpful. In particular, we would like to thank Logesh, along with a few other staff members whose names I unfortunately can’t recall, as well as Fandi from the Front Office.

Thank you, Capella, for the wonderful accommodations and exceptional service. It was truly a memorable stay.

text.text:
We chose Capella Singapore to celebrate our wedding anniversary, and somehow a simple staycation turned into a full-blown “are we secretly royalty?” experience. Honestly, if I could give 6 stars, I absolutely would.

I originally booked a Premier King Garden Room, but upon arrival we were unexpectedly upgraded to a One-Bedroom Villa — and the best part is, I didn’t even ask. That moment alone already set the tone for the stay: thoughtful, generous, and quietly impressive.

Service throughout the resort was impeccable. Every staff member we encountered was warm, friendly, and genuinely welcoming — the kind of hospitality that feels natural rather than scripted.

One of the fun perks here is the buggy service around the property. You can call for a buggy anytime to get around the resort. In fact, I called one to go to Fiamma, only to realise later that my villa was basically… right next to it. The ride took about 20 seconds. I felt a little embarrassed for making the driver come all the way just for that, but he greeted us with the biggest smile like it was the most normal thing in the world. That level of kindness really stuck with me.

The buggy can even take you all the way down to the carpark directly to your car, which is such a small but incredibly thoughtful touch.

From the beautiful grounds to the exceptional service, everything about this stay felt special. We had such a wonderful anniversary here, and it’s safe to say this won’t be our last visit.

Capella have officially set the bar very high for staycations. We’ll definitely be back. 🌿✨

For a budget hotel I was really surprised! The entire premises are kept really clean! Air conditioning was stellar! Super cold and no damp, mouldy smell whatsoever. The toilet/bathroom is small, but still quite comfortable to shower in. Doesn't feel cramped, even for someone who does feel claustrophobic in small spaces.

Despite being a little out of the way from vivocity and harbourfront, it was super easy to get to by either bus or a short walk. Pathways are decently lit and the road's lights easily illuminate everything else.

Staff were really nice upon check in (sorry I didn't get the staff's name). Really easy and quick check in, no issues whatsoever.

Definitely would stay again.

text.text:
Our stay at Equarius Hotel was peaceful, spacious, and very much centred around nature.

The room was generously sized and thoughtfully laid out, offering a quiet retreat away from the busier parts of Sentosa. Waking up to lush greenery created a calm, resort-like atmosphere that made the stay feel truly relaxing.

The pool area complemented this sense of tranquillity, surrounded by trees and greenery, it was uncrowded and ideal for a leisurely swim or simply unwinding.

One pleasant surprise was the gym. It was spacious, well equipped, and filled with natural light, with greenery visible all around. Working out here felt refreshing and unhurried, very different from the usual enclosed hotel gym experience.

Overall, Equarius Hotel is well suited for guests who value space, greenery, and a slower pace. A lovely choice for a restful staycation or a quiet escape within Sentosa.

text.text:
We had a 4 days 3 nights stay in Equarius Hotel and I have to say it is truly a 5 stars experience! Room feels new, big and clean. With big bathtub (however note that the bathtub has no handle so not suitable for young children alone or old people), and also nice balcony! The bed is also comfy and first time my husband never complain about the pillow! 😂 We love that they include everything in the mini bar and will refill daily, that’s normally only happens when you booked a suite. Everything in Equarius  Hotel (including the furnitures, fresh flowers, smell, even tea bags given inside room are TWG!) comes with quality! I truly appreciate that they decorated our room with some birthday perks and the gift box of chocolates was super delicious! 😋 (personally I find this idea much better than a cake as usually couldn’t finish and wasted) They even provide a nice box of child amenities! In summary, we had a very enjoyable stay and even the semi buffet breakfast were so great! Staff are very accommodating and great services! Me & my daughter fell down in Resort World, and they kindly provided us disinfectant tools and quality bandaids. I will give 5 stars to their facilities if they have children playground or indoor playground. Do note that their location is not near to beach. But the swimming pools are huge and nice.

Lastly, would like to special thanks to counter staff Steffanie for letting us check in early at 1pm without any extra charge! That was a lovely birthday surprise 😊

Check-in was 9/10 fast, and the staff was very friendly.

There are two places to eat for breakfast: Tangerine, right across from our hotel, and the other is located at the hotel, but requires effort due to the shuttle bus.

However, both were enjoyable and delicious.

As for the attractions, we received a 10% discount as guests.

The room itself was clean and had two cleaning sessions, which was great.

We will return to try other hotels.

from #831

text.text:
Our stay at the Ocean Suite at Resorts World Sentosa was nothing short of extraordinary, and much of that credit goes to our wonderful butler, William.

William was the definition of impeccable hospitality: dedicated, thoughtful, and genuinely caring. From the very beginning, he ensured everything was perfectly arranged, often before we even had to ask. His attention to detail, calm confidence, and warm demeanor made us feel completely at home while enjoying a world-class luxury experience. It’s rare to encounter someone who elevates a stay so effortlessly, and William truly did just that.

Of course, the Ocean Suite itself is breathtaking. The panoramic aquarium views are simply magical, watching the marine life swim gracefully past the windows, both day and night, is an experience that words can hardly do justice to. It’s serene, unique, and unforgettable.

This stay exceeded all expectations, and William’s exceptional service was the highlight that tied everything together. If you’re considering the Ocean Suite, you’re in for something truly special, especially if you’re lucky enough to have William as your butler.

text.text:
This hotel is pure magic — a stunning colonial-style haven filled with charm, tranquility, and timeless elegance. From the moment you step inside, you feel transported to another world — peaceful, refined, and surrounded by lush tropical beauty.

The vibe is truly special, with peacocks wandering gracefully around the gardens, adding to the enchanting atmosphere. I absolutely loved having direct access from my room to the pool, which was so convenient and luxurious. The private jacuzzi corner, tucked away behind tropical plants, felt like my own little oasis — peaceful, romantic, and completely private.

The staff are beyond exceptional — kind, thoughtful, and incredibly attentive. They genuinely care about every guest and make sure every detail is perfect. A very special thank you to Kady, Vivian, and Emily, who made my stay so memorable and heartwarming. Their warmth, smiles, and small gestures of care made me feel completely at home.

The breakfast was delicious, the high tea divine, and the evening apéro such a lovely touch — everything beautifully presented and served with such grace.

Located right on Sentosa, it’s the perfect little getaway from the city — close enough to everything, yet calm, private, and serene. You can easily reach the beach, Universal Studios, or explore the island before returning to your peaceful retreat.

Already dreaming of coming back. ✨

text.text:
We had an amazing time at The Laurus. The rooms were very new, clean and spacious. Staff were very prompt to help and welcoming.
We received welcome chocolates by Mr Bucket and a handwritten note from the Laurus team for our stay.
We dined and Laurus Table and the breakfast was AMAZING. Probably the best hotel breakfast I’ve experienced. The pool staff were welcoming as well. Though the hotel does not have a lounge for Premier Elite members, they do have other cool features - free mini bar, a WALK IN wardrobe, filtered drinking water tab and a Dyson hairdryer. The room slippers were so comfortable, I think it’s also one of the best hotel room slippers I had. Shower items like soap and shampoo were quality as well. To top it off, we had a complimentary Laurus owl plushy in the room too. The hotel being new was quiet, private and luxurious. Just what we were looking for. Hoping to come back one day for another staycay here.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

The hotel is brand new, clean, and feels properly luxurious without being stiff. Loved the big swimming pool and the landscaped area — perfect for relaxing after a long day out.

Big thanks to Mr. Victor, the Deputy Manager, for taking great care of us during the stay. Really attentive and made everything easy.

Would definitely stay here again. Highly recommended 👍

However, some guests mentioned that the hotel could improve on its lounge facilities for Premier Elite members and had limited wine selection. Others noted that the spa was not ready during their stay.

text.text:
The villa itself is exceptional — spacious, beautifully designed and easily superior to other properties we’ve stayed at in Singapore. The food was also excellent, and the setting was perfect for celebrating my parents’ birthdays (25 & 26 Feb).

Unfortunately, the butler service was a major disappointment. After a short buggy tour and receiving a contact card, there was no proactive contact at all. Not a single call during our stay, despite the service being presented as 24/7.

We had to chase for a pre-arranged birthday cake (booked since January), request and reconfirm our own late check-out, and even call reception ourselves when no buggy arrived at our confirmed 1:30pm departure. Housekeeping also turned up to clean the villa two hours after we had just checked in.

At check-out, the butler did not appear — only two plush toys were left at the counter, followed by an invoice email after we left.

For context, we have experienced excellent, proactive butler service at Marina Bay Sands, The Fullerton Hotel Singapore (Suites) and Sofitel Singapore Sentosa Resort & Spa (Villas).

Here, the service was disengaged and purely reactive.
Because of this, we decided not to extend our stay or dine at the on-site restaurants — which we had originally planned.

Amazing villa. Very disappointing butler service.

Rod S

The swimming pool is clean and well maintained, and very kid-friendly with a children’s wading pool and a lifeguard on duty, which gave us peace of mind. The Christmas booths at the hotel lobby added a warm and festive vibe to the stay.

This is our third time staying here, and we will definitely be back again. My kids enjoyed it a lot, making it a great choice for families.

text.text:
Location of 7-11 is very convenient!!

Rina managed to get us a great room. Marvellous view, facing the pool. She promptly directed us and even provided short-cuts to navigate around the hotel.

During check-in, the concierge — Diva & Adilah, were very warm and welcoming to my nephew. They treated my nephew with utmost care. Diva even provided a bag of colouring booklet and birthday balloons to my nephew!

Ashwini was very welcoming and friendly towards my nephew throughout our stay.

The tall Chinese uncle at the towel station was friendly and very accommodating to me and my nephew. He inflated a pool floatie and provided a water gun for my nephew too! It was a very pleasant interaction with him!

The staff at the breakfast counter, Sherry, were pleasant and very friendly. It was great to be greeted with such warmth in the mornings I had during the stay!

Upon checking out, I had issues in booking a taxi. Nabil was very helpful in placing a booking for us.

Razak frequently checked up on us to ensure that we were comfortable. He provided many recommendations and was swift with providing us what we needed.

Room for Improvement:
Please enlarge the symbols of pork on your food tags. I nearly consumed pork as the symbol was barely noticeable. I understand that Native Kitchen is not a fully Halal Certified dining restaurant, but I hope that VHS is able to make these changes to accommodate to their Muslim guests.

Tiles in the swimming pools should be replaced. The broken tiles are sharp and pose a risk to cuts. Hope that VHS will repair the tiles as soon as possible.

text.text:
Had a family staycation 2 weeks ago and it was a very enjoyable experience. Never had such a personalised customer service experience. From a week before my stay, they sent a Whatsapp to remind me about my stay and to ask what time is my ETA. They also provided a packing list based on what guests could do in the hotel and in Sentosa. A few days before my stay, Rina asked if I was celebrating any special occasion. On the day itself, Rina was all ready to receive us. In fact she had our room prepared to welcome my little ones, with balloons, snacks and activity kit greeting us.

Our room was facing the sea and we could see fireworks from the Wings of Times. We also enjoyed the movie screening on Fri and Sat night. Next to the hotel, there was also another movie screening by Movies by the Beach. The pools were so amazing! We enjoyed the pools so much!

The hotel is conveniently located next to Imbiah Station (5min walk away) and is linked to designated walkway to where all the beaches are.

Food wise, Vivo has a lot of food options. We were surprised that the food delivery is not so expensive as well.

Lovely place and lovely experience overall, and would definitely come back!

We’ve been coming to Siloso Beach Resort since 10 years ago or more — from 2 kids and now 3! It’s surrounded by lush greenery with a lovely spring water pool.

Around the pool, the towel station was unattended most of the time and sometimes it had no towel.

text.text:
Firstly, service here is top notch! It's always pleasant and welcoming, from Kathleen Grace of Front Office, Tara of Business Lounge, Leo of Pool Activities and every staff throughout our stay. Rasa Sentosa has always been our choice resort on Sentosa for its service, food quality, private beach & its activities though we couldn't be near the beach this time due to sightings of crocodile :(
Resort is perfectly located at the end of Siloso beach, close to the cable car, tram and buses, thou the resort has shuttle across to the mainland too.
The rooms have been refurbished and I miss the louver windows (maybe just me) thou its replaced with very thick blackout curtains.  Appreciated some of the eco initiatives such as aircon in the room shutting off when balcony doors are opened and turns it on when door's shut.  And among the comprehensive provision of toiletries, the good size toothpaste is packed separately from the toothbrush which was also a good size one...no waste of another toothbrush just to get to another tiny tube of toothpaste.
Room is well equipped with sufficient international power points, plentiful of drinking water and coffee & tea supplies, beach slippers and even a pretty tote bag. The water & ice dispenser in the corridor of every floor is godsend in this tropical resort.
Unfortunately, our sleep wasn't great.  Bed & pillows were uncomfortable. Sheets rustled "noisily" when tugged.  Also, TV resolution and bluetooth speaker on the side table were so terrible that they instantly make the resort felt cheap :(
Hope to return soon to a better experience.

We stayed at Shangri-La Rasa Sentosa for 11 days and had a wonderful time. The service was spectacular, everyone was friendly, attentive, and made the stay feel effortless.

We also used room service, which was convenient and consistently good. A really nice touch was the small goodies and little treats they left in our room,
those thoughtful details made us feel very welcomed.

It’s also incredibly family-oriented with lots to do for kids: a kids’ club, a kids’ pool, and plenty of activities, including on the beach. Our little ones enjoyed every single day during our stay.

Breakfast was another highlight, fantastic with many different options and something for everyone. Overall, a great choice for families and we’d happily return.

text.text:
Dear Management Team at Shangri-La Rasa Sentosa,
I am writing to share my feedback following a wonderful 3-day, 2-night stay at your resort. We had a fantastic experience and truly enjoyed the relaxing beachfront atmosphere.
I would like to especially highlight two members of your team who made our stay exceptional:
Jennifer Chung: Her professionalism, warmth, and attentiveness were truly impressive. She went above and beyond to ensure we were well taken care of.
Zul (Lifeguard): We really appreciated Zul’s friendly and approachable demeanor at the pool. It’s reassuring to see a safety professional who is not only vigilant but also so welcoming to guests.
Both Jennifer and Zul are a credit to the Shangri-La brand. Please ensure this praise is shared with them and their respective department heads.
Other highlights of our stay included:
The excellent breakfast spread and variety at Silver Shell Cafe.
The well-maintained pool facilities and the seamless beach access.
The overall "island escape" vibe that made our short getaway feel much longer.
Thank you for the great hospitality. We are already looking forward to our next visit!
Warm regards,
Hareedas

text.text:
I am sorry to write this review and I really wanted to like this place but I just could not.

I paid 5 star price for a 3 star quality hotel. No bell boy when we arrived at the hotel after 10pm. We had to drag our massive suitcases down, some stairs then onto elevator. The staff at reception were nice but NOT the 5 star service I was expecting. No proper welcome into their hotel like you expect to feel. The hotel/resort gave out no 5 star vibes. The breakfast was below average. I do have to mention that a young Indian ethnicity looking gentleman during breakfast may have been the only staff who had excellent service and communication but everyone else fell short from what I had expected.

The rooms were of 3.5 star to 4 star quality. I was so disappointed only because I paid over 750AUD per night for an absolute below average hotel. Often when I called reception for waters, there was no answer. On our departure date I called multiple times to no avail and I was forced to walk down to lobby to request for bell boy.

My apologies for this review once again but paying so much really left a dent on this experience.

The staff at reception were nice but NOT the 5 star service I was expecting. No proper welcome into their hotel like you expect to feel. The hotel/resort gave out no 5 star vibes. The breakfast was below average. I do have to mention that a young Indian ethnicity looking gentleman during breakfast may have been the only staff who had excellent service and communication but everyone else fell short from what I had expected.

The rooms were of 3.5 star to 4 star quality. I was so disappointed only because I paid over 750AUD per night for an absolute below average hotel. Often when I called reception for waters, there was no answer. On our departure date I called multiple times to no avail and I was forced to walk down to lobby to request for bell boy.

My apologies for this review once again but paying so much really left a dent on this experience.

text.text:
I had an opportunity to work in the yacht industry for part of my career path. And I was lucky to meet an elderly couple (they were probably in their 70s) who had passed on a very important lesson to me - managing our emotions.

We had an event with some car brands and they came in an entourage. It was a nice event, everybody had a great time. But they had some issues with their car and it was past midnight 1 or 2 am? Everybody had left, I stayed with them until help came.

I know they were probably a bit annoyed /tired because it was a really nice car from an established brand. But they remained very calm and collected. Thank you K family 😘

text.text:
I had our solemnization and lunch reception here at the Nova room and we are truly happy with the service and coordination.

Love the location and ambience of the Nova room as well as the location. Additionally, the prompt response from Beck Chin is one of the important aspect we have decided on the location.

Prior to contacting ONE°15 Marina, we have contacted several other hotels. However, most of the time, we were met with slow response, unlike Beck Chin, he has been very prompt in response right from the start of our enquiries till after the event. He has been a great help in coordinating our needs and gave good suggestions along the way.

Perhaps, the room is slightly below our expectation. The lighting is pretty dim and relatively basic. The hefty deposit is rather uncommon as hotels would typically use credit card as guarantee. Additionally, the hotel should consider to provide flexibility  for early check-ins for bridal make up as that would would certainly be helpful for couples having lunch reception.

Again, thanks Beck Chin for the help to create a memorable event for us.

text.text:
Recently held my wedding at ONE°15 Marina and me and the wife's experience was immaculate.

My first contact was with Beck. From the start up till the very end, he was responsive and attentive to our needs. He would reply us almost immediately anytime of the day and would follow up with any of the unknowns and queries expeditiously. Beck is also knowledgeable and sensitive to cultural/religious norms; a major plus for our multicultural wedding affair! Thank you Beck, we had a good feeling after we first met you and we were definitely not wrong!

On our big day, banquet manager Joyston was assigned to us. I had met him the day before for coordination and I must say I was impressed by his grasp on our wedding itinerary. He was able to remember specific details regarding the events! Along with the bridal team, groomsmen and vendors he was able to facilitate a smooth flow of events. Although it was a short engagement with Joyston, we are thankful that we had experienced the amazing service you provided.

The backdrop of the yatches and boats along with the decor provided made for a beautiful venue. The rooms provided for us, though just slightly dated in certain aspects, were very clean and comfortable. Service provided by staff was timely and efficient.

Thank you ONE°15 Marina for an extremely memorable wedding experience :)

text.text:
This is our third time staying at Furama RiverFront whenever we visit Singapore, and it has always been a great experience. Traveling with kids can sometimes be challenging, but this hotel makes everything much easier.

The rooms are spacious, which is very helpful when traveling with big luggage and family belongings. There’s plenty of space, so we never feel cramped even with the kids.

The staff are friendly and helpful, which always makes our stay comfortable. The location is also very convenient — it’s close to Havelock MRT Station and within walking distance to Great World Mall and MRT, making it easy to get around and find food or do some shopping.

Overall, it’s a very family-friendly hotel and we always enjoy staying here whenever we visit Singapore.

text.text:
We stayed at the Furama RiverFront Hotel and found that it met all our needs during our trip. While the property is a little dated in some areas, it’s still comfortable and offered good value overall. The staff were friendly, helpful, and provided excellent service whenever we needed assistance. A big shout out to the older gentleman on the door , what a legend.
Housekeeping is scheduled every four days, which worked fine for us, though some guests may prefer more frequent cleaning. Saying that we could restock coffee , sugar , towels and toilet paper as we needed outside each lift on every floor. The on-site facilities were a nice bonus — a swimming pool, tennis court, and even a self-service laundry room ($8 per wash and $8 per dry), which made things very convenient.
The buffet breakfast at $15 and dinner at $30 (as of 2026) were well worth it, offering generous portions and good variety. Overall, a practical and pleasant choice for travellers seeking comfort, friendly service, and good amenities at a reasonable price.

text.text:
Had a couple of nights in a Club room. First of all, the location is top: it’s away from the crowds but still in a convenient part of the downtown area. We walked to both Chinatown and Marina Bay.

The club level is really great, with a nice, relaxing pool area. I liked being able to grab something from the lounge and enjoy it by the pool. Evening cocktails were nice, especially on Christmas Eve, with fantastic food and snack offerings and excellent service. We preferred having breakfast at the club level.

The room, however, was not the best. The interior felt worn. It took a long time to get hot water, and the shower drain was very slow. The Wi-Fi stopped working one evening. The AC blasted cold air straight onto the bed.

Overall, I would still recommend staying here, thanks to the location, club level benefits and professional staff.

text.text:
Good stay at Oasia Hotel Downtown - I stayed on the club floor of the hotel and had a positive experience. The rooms are clean and spacious albeit a bit dated and dark. The location is great, 2mins from MRT and close to lots of eateries and attractions.

I’m not sure if paying extra for the club floor was worth it for me - I preferred the pool on the top (non club) floor as it had more sunshine. Club breakfasts were good but more vegetarian options could be provided. I was mostly out and about in the evenings so only tried their snacks once (again, more veggie options would be great!). I was eager to try pilates but it wasn’t scheduled on the days I was staying and you need to book 2 days in advance.

The highlight of my stay was Lee the housekeeper on the 24th floor - such a friendly and kind person!

I may stay again in the future but likely in the non-club part of the hotel :)

text.text:
The hotel is is conviently located next to the metro.

We spent our first few nights on the club floors which had it's own pool area and breakfast lounge. The club room is slightly bigger and has a bath but the bathroom is completely see through, so if you enjoy watching your partner take a dump this is the room for you.

The standard room is slightly smaller but the bathroom is private.

The breakfast was very poor for an Asian hotel and I'd recommend not taking it.

There seems to be some maintenance service and issues at this hotel, both rooms felt slightly worn/lacking maintenance in places. The communal areas often had plates and glasses lying around in the evening. The bathroom has a speak but I think they don't work in any of the rooms.

The worst was the internet and WiFi which was intermittent and extremely slow, it made working near impossible and really isn't acceptable.

The hotel is incredibly designed but it lacks the polish of the big chains and these guys need to get their act together if they expect to compete with the likes of IHG etc.

Overall you get a very good location and room and the prices would probably be higher if they could have a more polished product. They spent a lot of money o nthe building but are not managing it properly.

text.text:
Location is excellent in the edge of Chinatown. A few blocks to two main MRT lines I used to get around Singapore.

Room was on the small size but fine for 2 adults. Bed was comfortable. Liked there was 2 usb outlets on both sides of the beds. Received a room with a floor to ceiling window with an excellent view of Chinatown. Breakfast at an extra cost was delicious with Asian and Western options.

A few negatives: Limited TV channels. It seemed like the room was missing a nightstand on one side as there was room for one. Bathroom was small but laid out well. Issue was with small bugs coming out of the drains, but didn't bother us enough to complain. Room was generally quiet but I did hear drum banging for a few minutes at 7am every morning - think it was a temple's morning ritual.

Staff was courteous and professional.

text.text:
I had an extremely unpleasant stay at this hotel.

When I checked in, I asked the front desk for an extra blanket for my family. They told me the hotel was fully booked and had no extra duvet available, even though the booking app clearly showed there were still rooms for sale.

The staff also insisted that a twin room only comes with two duvets and that they do not provide extra duvrt as a rule. I tried to explain but got no help.

I then contacted the booking platform’s customer service, who followed up with the hotel. The hotel’s reply was still the same: no extra duvet, not even for a fee.

This is unbelievable and completely unacceptable. It’s the first time I’ve ever heard of a hotel charging for an extra duvet. What’s more, how can a hotel run out of duvets entirely? It’s shocking that a so-called professional hotel has no spare supplies at all.

We are a family of three, including an 8-year-old child. The room was cold at night, and we only needed one more duvet to keep warm. Is that too much to ask?

I used to think Far East Organization was a benchmark in hotel management, but this experience has put this hotel straight on my blacklist. I will not recommend this hotel to anyone.

The hotel is beautiful & the service was great. The rooftop pool is incredible! Breakfast was very good also.

The rooms are nice and well laid out. Our only pitfall which falls under service and room was we were placed above ‘The Jungle Room’ cocktail bar, Wednesday - Saturday this is open until 2am. We were staying with a 5 month old baby, I am not sure why this room was allocated to us. The hotel was full of childless people probably attending the night ( it looked good!), it probably would be better suited to this clientele. We also had no cot on arrival for our baby.

The location is excellent, close to china town and good access to the train links. When we travelled on the train they were almost empty I’d definitely recommend this mode of transport for getting around the city (outside the airport).

Service from the concierge team was amazing, they were always so helpful with recommendations and taxis.

text.text: Our stay at Mondrian Singapore Duxton was incredible, and provided the perfect base for our stay in Singapore. In the heart of Chinatown, with many brilliant places to eat nearby and very well connected through various MRT stations, there was much to explore locally but also very easy to venture further out. With a rooftop pool & bar, daily happy hour, fantastic in-house catering and a well stocked gym, the hotel was the perfect place to relax before going out or after returning from a busy day of site seeing. The room was stunning, as were the views. Clean, spacious and comfortable. Staff were kind, professional and incredibly hospitable. Especially the hotel's assistant manager, Miss Soo Lai Yin. We are very grateful to Mondrian Singapore Duxton for such a wonderful stay, and we look forward to returning in the near future.

text.text:
We just spent 8 nights at the Furama City Centre hotel and though i do not usually give reviews, I feel that it would be remiss of me not to provide a very positive review for this hotel.
As a starting point, the location is just the BEST...sheltered walkways to shopping mall and MRT station..it rained everyday and we had no issue moving about because of the connectivity with the sheltered walkway...genius:)
HUGE shout out to Zacus Chua (concierge) and Pauline (unsure of her name, she was the front desk who checked us into the hotel). The staff at this hotel is amazing, super helpful, always going the extra mile..being in the hospitality industry myself, I would give them 10 stars if that was available. So impressed with their warm, caring, helpful, can-do  attitude. No task is too hard, impressive.
Yes, the hotel is dated, rooms and bathroom are dated, but,beds are very comfortable,  spacious enough. Even though the room cleaning happens only every 3 days, the hotel provides all the amenities at the lift lobby where guests can collect fresh towels and amenities anytime. I have ZERO issue with the 3 day cleaning service. And on the subject of the cleaning, I did not get the name of the lady that does the cleaning on our floor (17floor) but she does an IMPECCABLE job of cleaning, not a speck of dirt left on the carpet or sink after she's been through. Honestly, she has got to be the most thorough and hardworking housekeeping staff that i have ever met in my whole life of travelling (and being senior citizens, that's alot of travelling)
One thing i can say is, I would recommend without hesitation, this is the hotel I would return to on all my annual trips to Singapore, pity we didn't find this hotel any earlier.
Hoestly, all the Hyatts and Hiltons cannot compare to this hotel...though it is not rated a 5 star hotel, I would rate it a 8 star hotel (if there was such a classification) for comfort, location, staff can do attitude...
I do hope the management of the Furama City Centre hotel will give the mentioned staff a bonus for the great work they did to form such a memorable stay for us.

text.text:
What a gem in the centre of Singapore. Booked an Executive Club room for a week. The room (2008) has been renovated and was large and very comfortable. Breakfast was included and you were spoiled for choice. A shout out to Alice in the dining room for organising my Gluten Free Bread each morning.
Drinks and light food was available in the Executive Lounge each evening between 6pm and 8pm, spirits, wine, beer and soft drink’s included. The staff in the lounge were so much fun and made every guest feel special, Ralph, Ray and Ivy were all so lovely and kept the drinks flowing.
Location is amazing 10min walk to China Town and 10 min walk to Clarke Quay, the MRT was a 5min walk on the Downtown Line, MRT is the only way to get around.
The pool on level 6 is very nice once you find it and the Gym looked well equipped. There is a self serve laundry on level 6 as well, a bit pricey but convenient.
I would definitely recommend and would stay again when next in Singapore.

Our family had a very pleasant stay at this hotel. We were traveling with two children, and right from the check-in process, the staff made us feel warmly welcomed. As a thoughtful gesture, they gave our kids two adorable hotel plush toys, which immediately brought big smiles to their faces. It was a small detail, but it made a wonderful first impression and showed how attentive and considerate the staff were toward families.

The hotel itself was very clean and well-maintained. Everything in the room was tidy, comfortable, and organized, allowing us to relax after a long day of exploring the city. The staff throughout our stay were friendly, helpful, and always ready to assist with any questions we had. Their positive attitude added to the overall comfort of our experience. Most importantly, the location of the hotel is excellent. Chinatown MRT station is just downstairs, making it extremely convenient to travel anywhere in the city. This unbeatable location saved us a lot of time and made getting around with two children much easier. Overall, we had a comfortable and enjoyable stay and would gladly recommend this hotel to other families.

Staff is really friendly and helpful. Helped me carry my baggage, offered to call taxi, etc. Rooms are really good size and comfy, complimentary drinks and snacks are a nice touch. Condition of rooms and furniture is ok but it looks a bit over-used in some areas, not  a big deal, really. However, the thing that kills the final score is the fact that I had large (thumb-sized) bugs in my bathroom each day. Get rid of one, another one appears. Further, found a little roommate in the shape of a (small) hand-sized lizard on day two. Went into the closet, never saw him leave. Called him Steve, hope he is ok.

text.text:
Had a course in SGH and stayed in this hotel as it was closeby.
Location wise, near 2 MRT stations about 5-10 min walk. Located in Tiong Bharu area which has plenty of food. The tiong bharu hawker is very close too.
Rooms were clean, had basic tea and coffee.
Only thing is there was no breakfast provided in this hotel. That was kind of a let down. They did provide some snacks and water at the lobby.
In preparation of no breakfast, the hotel supplied a small kitchen area in the lobby, with a microwave, kettle and water filter dispenser. Outside the hotel, there were 2 vending machines selling instant noodles and snacks.
I guess they expect guests to eat noodles for breakfast or go to the hawker centre nearby.
Overall this hotel is great value for guests who want to spend time exploring the nearby areas.
It was also fully booked when i checked out showing it was a popular choice among travellers

text.text:
Kitty and I recently decided to enjoy a staycation at Copthorne King's Hotel Singapore, motivated by a special promotion on Agoda offering rooms at $137 through my membership. The hotel is well established and located along Havelock Road, close to the vibrant Robertson Quay area and just a five minute walk from Havelock MRT station, providing easy access to Chinatown, Orchard Road, and the Central Business District.

Upon arrival around 5:30 PM, we encountered a large crowd of tourists checking in. There were multiple self help and quick check in kiosks, which we decided to use. However, the self check in system was not very user friendly. It required scanning a physical IC card rather than digital options like Singpass. The scanner struggled to capture our details correctly, necessitating multiple attempts. Once we finally succeeded, the system prompted us for an additional payment despite our booking being fully paid via Agoda, with the charge already processed on my credit card. As a result, we had to rejoin the long queue at the main counter to resolve the issue, and the entire check in took about 30 minutes.

It would be advisable to note that for guest  that a physical IC is required for check in, as digital options like Singpass are not accepted. Clear communication of this requirement would help avoid unnecessary delays and confusion.

During our stay, I suggested to the staff that the kiosks should include a note indicating that they are not suitable for guests who have paid online in advance to prevent such inconveniences.

Our room was well maintained despite the hotel's age, spacious, and featured a small balcony with a pleasant city view. The bathroom and toiletries were sufficient, and the modern TV supported Chromecast and screen mirroring, providing convenient in room entertainment.

The hotel offers amenities such as a pool on level three, sauna, steam bath, and gym. While we didn’t use these facilities, they appeared to be well maintained. We also took some photos to preserve the memories.

On level one, there is a space with a Starbucks self service coffee machine, providing a convenient spot to buy coffee and relax.  There’s also a lounge with a live band that normally features performances, but during our visit at night, there were no activities at the lounge. The cafeteria  was under renovation during our stay therfore there are no breakfast.

Location wise, the hotel is excellent for exploring the city centre. However, within immediate walking distance, there isn’t much to do. For attractions like Tiong Bahru and Club Street, a short walk or Grab ride is recommended, both of which offer diverse dining and entertainment options.

A disappointing moment was at around 11 PM when Kitty queued at the reception to request a check-out extension from 11 AM to 12 PM. When it was her turn, a staff member,  likely the manager,  briefly acknowledged us but then turned away and left without explanation, entering his office and not returning. A simple apology or notification of his relevant absence would have been more courteous.

In summary, Copthorne King's Hotel Singapore still remains a solid choice with modern amenities, good location, and comfortable rooms. Nevertheless, there is room for improvement in the check in process, staff communication, and guest service. Addressing these issues could greatly enhance the guest experience. We would consider staying again in the future.

text.text:
We got married on 17 Jan 2026 at Copthorne King’s. It went very well and we enjoyed ourselves.

The food was delicious, and many guests praised it. Even though we were too busy during the wedding to eat properly, the hotel staff was kind enough to help us pack the food, and it still tasted amazing after a few hours.

The programme went smoothly from the tea ceremony, to the cocktail, to the lunch reception. The banquet managers were on point and patient with us, even when we had to push back the schedule. Special shoutout to Desmond, Alvin, Lim, and our sales coordinator Adrian, who patiently accommodated our requests and many last minute changes. Adrian also helped us secure a special pureed diet for one of our guests, which made it possible for her to attend and celebrate with us. Thank you for making us feel so taken care of throughout the entire day.

The banquet hall and tea ceremony hall were also nicely decorated, and we could really feel the cozy, warm atmosphere. Our room stays were fantastic too. The rooms were comfortable, and it was such a nice space for us to rest and recharge in between the day’s events.

Thank you for taking such good care of us and our guests. We’re truly grateful, and we would happily recommend your team to other couples.

text.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I had a fantastic stay at Copthorne King’s Hotel in Singapore. The value for money was incredible – I paid just £80 per night and booked two rooms, which felt like an absolute bargain for the location and quality.

One room had a beautiful view over the road towards the Intercontinental, which was lovely to wake up to. The other overlooked the pool and a quieter side of the hotel, which was peaceful and relaxing. Both rooms were spotless and very well maintained. Housekeeping was excellent throughout our stay. I also loved the AI assistant radio in the room – such a cool touch and something a bit different.

We did have a slight wobble at check-in as we were initially met by someone at reception who was not particularly welcoming. However, after I raised this with management, it was resolved within minutes. I received a very kind apology and a beautiful fruit platter was sent up to our room almost immediately. The way management handled the situation completely turned things around and is exactly why this is a five-star review. I really appreciated how quickly and professionally it was dealt with, and it genuinely made a difference.

The facilities are great. The pool area is really nice, with a jacuzzi and sauna, plus a lovely, well-equipped gym. Very clean as well. The location is perfect – right in the centre, two stops from Orchard Road, and within easy reach of Chinatown and Clarke Quay. You would be silly not to stay here.

I would definitely come back. Fantastic value, great facilities and excellent management response when it mattered most.

I honestly don’t understand how this hotel qualifies as a four-star property. The concierge staff welcomed guests with unfriendly expressions and lacked basic courtesy. There was no greeting, no smile, and no effort to provide essential information such as directions to the lift, the guest room, or the breakfast area.

The room was small, and the worst part was that the glass window was covered with green algae!!! completely unacceptable for a hotel of this rating. We requested housekeeping service in the morning, but when we returned to our room in the late afternoon, no one had come. The bathroom door was faulty and couldn’t be locked, and to make matters worse, the shower gel bottle was completely empty! clearly not checked or refilled by housekeeping before our arrival. It’s also surprising that a supposed four-star hotel does not provide complimentary bottled water.

Overall, this experience was extremely disappointing. I never expected to encounter such poor standards in Singapore, a country known for its hospitality and high service quality. This hotel truly falls short of Singapore’s reputation, and I would not recommend it to anyone.

The bathroom door was faulty and couldn’t be locked, and to make matters worse, the shower gel bottle was completely empty! clearly not checked or refilled by housekeeping before our arrival. It’s also surprising that a supposed four-star hotel does not provide complimentary bottled water.

Overall, this experience was extremely disappointing. I never expected to encounter such poor standards in Singapore, a country known for its hospitality and high service quality. This hotel truly falls short of Singapore’s reputation, and I would not recommend it to anyone.
